title: Safe Pipe
description: Angular pipe that wraps DomSanitizer to mark trusted HTML, styles, scripts, URLs, and resource URLs as safe for template binding — used throughout AcoreX documentation and demo components.

hints.title: Security first
hints.content: The safe pipe calls bypassSecurityTrust* — it disables Angular's XSS protection. Only use it for content you fully trust (your own CMS, sanitized API, static docs). Never pipe unsanitized user input.

hints.title: Import the pipe in standalone components
hints.content: Add AXSafePipe to the component imports array. The empty PipesModule is not required.

hints.title: Pipe name is safe
hints.content: Use in templates as {{ value | safe: 'html' }} — the pipe name is safe, not axSafe.

hints.title: Empty values
hints.content: When value is null, undefined, or an empty string, the pipe returns '' without calling the sanitizer.

types.title: AXSafePipe
types.description: Pure pipe delegating to Angular DomSanitizer bypass methods.

methods.content:
/**
 * Marks a value as trusted for Angular binding.
 *
 * @param value - Raw string to trust.
 * @param type - One of: 'html' | 'style' | 'script' | 'url' | 'resourceUrl'
 * @returns SafeHtml | SafeStyle | SafeScript | SafeUrl | SafeResourceUrl
 * @throws Error when type is not recognized
 */
transform(value: unknown, type: string): SafeHtml | SafeStyle | SafeScript | SafeUrl | SafeResourceUrl | ''

types.title: Safe type reference
types.description: Which DomSanitizer method each type maps to.

props.name: url
props.description: For href, img src, and similar URL bindings. bypassSecurityTrustUrl().

props.name: resourceUrl
props.description: For iframe src, object/embed, etc. bypassSecurityTrustResourceUrl().

content.data:
The @acorex/core/pipes package currently exports AXSafePipe — a thin, reusable wrapper around Angular's DomSanitizer.

Angular sanitizes bound values by default to prevent XSS. When you need to render trusted HTML from documentation JSON, embed demo iframes, or display rich hint callouts, Angular blocks the binding unless the value is explicitly marked safe.

AXSafePipe centralizes that pattern so you do not repeat DomSanitizer.inject() boilerplate in every component.

sections.description: Import AXSafePipe in a standalone component, then choose the safe type that matches the Angular binding target.

sections.title: Security Guidelines
sections.description: When to use and when to avoid.

content.data:
Safe to use when:

• Content comes from your own documentation JSON or CMS you control
• SVG/HTML is generated server-side with sanitization
• iframe URLs are whitelisted demo paths on your domain

Avoid when:

• Rendering raw user comments, chat messages, or form input
• Binding URLs from unvalidated query parameters
• Trusting third-party API HTML without server-side sanitization

Alternatives for untrusted HTML:
• Sanitize on the server with a library like DOMPurify before storing
• Use Angular's default sanitization (no bypass) for partial trust
• Display plain text instead of innerHTML

sections.title: Safe Type Cheat Sheet
sections.description: Quick reference for choosing the right type.

| Template binding | Pipe type | Example | |------------------|-----------|--------| | [innerHTML] | html | `
` | | [style] / style.* | style | `
` | | [src] on iframe | resourceUrl | `` | | [src] on img | url | `` | | [href] | url | `` | | [src] on script | script | Rare — avoid if possible | Invalid type strings throw: `Error: Invalid safe type specified: ...`
